introSection: Gao (Japanese: ガオー) is a steel roller coaster located at Greenland in Arao, Kumamoto, Japan. It was built by Meisho Amusement Machines and opened in 1998.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Gao originally opened as Hurricane (Japanese: ハリケーン) on 8 March 1979. This version of the ride featured a different layout, including a vertical loop and missing the iconic dinosaur sculpture that now dawns the ride.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: The coaster uses different colours for the track these being; light blue, orange, mint green, light purple, white and yellow. The supports are white.

fullText:
Gao (Japanese: ガオー) is a steel roller coaster located at Greenland in Arao, Kumamoto, Japan. It was built by Meisho Amusement Machines and opened in 1998.

Gao originally opened as Hurricane (Japanese: ハリケーン) on 8 March 1979. This version of the ride featured a different layout, including a vertical loop and missing the iconic dinosaur sculpture that now dawns the ride.



The coaster uses different colours for the track these being; light blue, orange, mint green, light purple, white and yellow. The supports are white.




 * Gao on the parks webstie (Japanese)
